commit.message:
fix load data report 'unsupported cast from json to JSON' (#23825)

Fix LOAD DATA ... PARALLEL 'TRUE' failure on tables with JSON columns and irregular indexes (for example IVFFLAT).

This change adds a safe identity cast path for JSON -> JSON, which prevents parallel load from failing with:

internal error: unsupported cast from json to JSON
Root Cause
For LOAD DATA on tables with irregular indexes, the planner may fall back to the legacy load path.
In the parallel branch, that path introduces an extra cast layer. For JSON columns, execution could reach the generic jsonToOthers() cast path.

Before this change, jsonToOthers() did not handle JSON -> JSON, so parallel load could fail even though the source and target types were both JSON.

Fix
Add an explicit identity cast branch for JSON -> JSON in pkg/sql/plan/function/func_cast.go
Copy JSON values safely via GetStrValue()/AppendBytes() instead of using a generic fixed-type duplication path
Add a regression unit test for JSON -> JSON cast in pkg/sql/plan/function/func_cast_test.go
Impact
This fixes parallel LOAD DATA for scenarios like:

JSON column present
IVFFLAT index present
LOAD DATA URL S3OPTION ... PARALLEL 'TRUE'
The fix is intentionally minimal and does not change the broader legacy load path behavior.

Files Changed
pkg/sql/plan/function/func_cast.go
pkg/sql/plan/function/func_cast_test.go
Validation
Unit Test
go test ./pkg/sql/plan/function -run 'TestCastJsonTo(Json|Numeric)' -count=1
Build
make build
Runtime Verification
Verified with a real LOAD DATA URL S3OPTION ... PARALLEL 'TRUE' flow against local MinIO.

Scenario:

table with JSON column
VECF64 column
IVFFLAT index
large CSV file (parallel path actually triggered)
Result:

before fix: internal error: unsupported cast from json to JSON
after fix: parallel load succeeded

Notes
This PR is a minimal functional fix to unblock the reported issue.
If needed, the legacy parallel LOAD DATA cast path can be further cleaned up in a follow-up change.

Approved by: @aunjgr

commit.message:
fix: make tenant SHOW TABLE STATUS rows consistent for system statement_info (#23774)

Background / Root Cause
This PR fixes issue #10163: under a tenant account, SHOW TABLE STATUS for system.statement_info could show Rows = 0 (or otherwise inconsistent values) even when tenant-visible rows existed.

Root cause:

SHOW TABLE STATUS originally used mo_table_rows(db, tbl) for Rows.
For special system tables (for example system.statement_info), tenant queries are rewritten to run under the sys account with tenant-level filtering.
mo_table_rows does not always reflect that rewritten tenant-visible result set, so Rows could be inaccurate.
Solution
In handleShowTableStatus, add a targeted fallback path:

For non-sys accounts, and only for tables where ShouldSwitchToSysAccount(db, tbl) is true, run select count(*) on that table.
Use this count(*) result to override the Rows column in SHOW TABLE STATUS.
Keep existing behavior for all other tables (mo_table_rows/mo_table_size).
Route internal SQL execution through ExeSqlInBgSes (wrapper) to make stubbing in unit tests straightforward.
Test Coverage
Unit Tests ([mysql_cmd_executor_test.go](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.73-darwin-x64/webview/#))
Added/covered boundary cases:

Fallback success path: mo_table_rows=0, count(*)>0, Rows is correctly overridden.
Non-special table: fallback is not triggered.
Sys account: fallback is not triggered.
Empty count(*) result: keep mo_table_rows.
count(*) execution error path.
count(*) decode (GetInt64) error path.
BVT (test/distributed/cases/dml/show/show7.sql/.result)
Added issue regression case with stabilization:

Tenant-session warm-up statements + wait (sleep) to avoid async log timing flakiness.
Pre-check count(*) > 0 on system.statement_info.
Validate SHOW TABLE STATUS ... LIKE 'statement_info' returns positive Rows (regex-based assertion).
Scope / Risk
Behavior change is limited to: non-sys account + special system tables requiring sys-account switch.
Adds one extra count(*) query only in that narrow path.
All other SHOW TABLE STATUS behavior remains unchanged.

Approved by: @heni02, @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
fix: prevent nil-context panic in internal SQL executor (#17630) (#23821)

This PR fixes issue #17630, where importing DDLs could trigger panic: cannot create context from nil parent during internal SQL execution.

Root cause
Some internal SQL paths assumed the execution context was always non-nil. In DDL-related flows, especially when internal metadata queries were triggered during table creation, proc.Ctx could be nil. That nil context was then passed into code paths using context.WithValue(...), which caused the panic.

Fix
The fix adds two layers of protection:

In Compile.runSqlWithResultAndOptions, fall back from proc.Ctx to proc.GetTopContext(), and finally to context.Background() if needed.
In sqlExecutor, normalize nil contexts at executor entry points, so internal SQL execution never operates on a nil parent context.
Validation

Added unit tests covering both the compile-side fallback and the internal SQL executor nil-context case.
Added a SQL regression case for the issue scenario under test/distributed/cases/foreign_key/issue_17630.sql.
Verified with go test ./pkg/sql/compile, make build, standalone runtime SQL execution, and mo-tester for the new regression case.

Approved by: @aunjgr, @heni02, @ouyuanning

commit.message:
feat: implement null-safe equal operator (<=>) (#23599)

Summary
This PR implements the NULL-safe equal operator (<=>), providing full compatibility with MySQL semantics. It ensures that NULL <=> NULL returns 1 (TRUE) and Value <=> NULL returns 0 (FALSE), never returning NULL.

Changes
- Function Registration: Added NULL_SAFE_EQUAL to function_id.go and initialized metadata in init.go and list_operator.go.
- Execution Logic: Implemented vectorized nullSafeEqualFn in func_compare.go with support for all major data types (Numeric, String, JSON, Decimal, Date/Time, etc.).
- Plan & Binding:
- Updated base_binder.go to bind tree.NULL_SAFE_EQUAL to the <=> function.
- Implemented Tuple expansion logic: (a, b) <=> (c, d) is automatically expanded to (a <=> c) AND (b <=> d).
- Optimizer Integration: Marked the operator as PRODUCE_NO_NULL, allowing the optimizer to deduce NotNullable properties and perform optimizations like IS NULL constant folding.
- Safety & Correctness:
- Join: Explicitly excluded from equi-join identification (IsEqualFunc) to avoid incorrect result dropping in the current Hash
Join implementation regarding NULL keys.
- Zonemap: Disabled ZONEMAPPABLE flag to prevent incorrect block pruning, as the generic evaluator currently lacks specialized logic for NULL-safe comparisons.

Testing
- Added comprehensive BVT cases in test/distributed/cases/function/func_null_safe_equal.sql.
- Coverage:
- Basic scalar comparisons with all NULL combinations.
- Table-based filtering and Join correctness (NULL matching NULL).
- Complex types: JSON, Decimal (with scale alignment), and Date/Time.
- Tuple comparisons and nested logic.
- Optimizer attribute verification (verifying results are never NULL).
- All tests verified against a live MatrixOne instance.

Approved by: @aunjgr, @heni02, @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
fix(plan): enforce contiguous prefix matching for composite secondary index filters (#23818)

This PR fixes a wrong-result issue in index optimization for composite secondary indexes.

When querying a composite index like (uid, typ, flag) with predicates such as uid = 'alice' AND flag = 1
(missing middle key typ), the optimizer incorrectly treated non-contiguous keys as a valid leading prefix and
built an invalid prefix_eq condition on the index table. This could return 0 rows incorrectly.

### Root cause

tryMatchMoreLeadingFilters kept matching later index parts even after an intermediate part was not found,
violating the contiguous-prefix rule for composite indexes.

### What changed

1. Updated tryMatchMoreLeadingFilters to stop matching as soon as any intermediate index part is missing.
2. Added a regression test to ensure only contiguous leading parts are matched.

Approved by: @ouyuanning, @heni02

commit.message:
txnbase: split wal/apply pipeline queues for txn commit path (#23806)

### Summary
- split txn commit pipeline from single `walAndApplyQueue` into `preWalQueue -> walQueue -> applyQueue`
- keep transaction semantics unchanged while increasing pipeline parallelism
- remove temporary queue diagnostic sampling/logging and keep only functional change

### Motivation
- sysbench `oltp_delete` (100 threads, 10 * 10M rows) regressed after queue model changes
- this pipeline split restores throughput in the reproduced workload

### Scope
- txnbase queue wiring and stage handlers only
- no SQL-layer or protocol behavior changes

### Testing
- validated in internal sysbench reproduction workflow (same workload above)
- note: local full build is environment-dependent due to third-party headers/libraries

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
fix issue 20022 (#23740)

Problem
Under long-running load with proxy connection cache enabled, backend sessions (especially dump) kept accumulating, eventually causing login failures and instability.

Root Cause

QUIT handling was asynchronous (fire-and-forget), so cleanup was not guaranteed before the handler exited.
In handler defer logic, when connection cache was enabled, backend connections could be left open even if they were not successfully cached.
On EOF/connection-end paths (client disconnect without COM_QUIT), there was no explicit fallback cleanup.
Together, these paths leaked backend connections over time.
Fix

Make quit cleanup synchronous and idempotent in clientConn using [sync.Once](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.71-darwin-x64/webview/#).
Track whether a backend connection was actually cached (cached flag).
In handler finalization:
keep backend connection only if it was successfully cached;
otherwise force cleanup via Quit() (fallback to Close()).
Add EOF/conn-end fallback cleanup by explicitly triggering the quit path once.
Why this is minimal

Changes are limited to proxy connection lifecycle/cleanup paths.
No changes to SQL execution, routing policy, or auth semantics.
Validation

Environment: local 2 CN + proxy, conn-cache enabled.
Workload: point_select_10_1000000_prepare, 300 terminals, 70 minutes.
Result: ERROR=0, SUCCEED.
dump connections stabilized (no continuous growth after warm-up), including past the previous ~57-minute failure window.
Repeated login checks succeeded; no panic observed.

Approved by: @gouhongshen, @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
fix: data branch create table + DROP DATABASE race condition (#23788)

Two-layer fix for orphan records in mo_tables caused by concurrent data branch create table (CLONE) and DROP DATABASE, which leads to ExpectedEOB panic on checkpoint replay.

Fix 1 - Lock Service: Lock mode sync on holder promotion
Lock is a Go value type. When addHolder is called during tryHold,
modifications to l.value (lock mode byte) don't propagate to the
store. Add setMode() method to correctly update the stored lock
entry's mode when a waiter is promoted to holder (newHolder=true).
This ensures Exclusive locks actually block Shared requests.

Files: lock.go, lock_table_local.go, types.go

Fix 2 - DDL Layer: Snapshot refresh after exclusive lock (clock.Now)
After acquiring exclusive lock on mo_database in DropDatabase, call
refreshSnapshotAfterLock which uses clock.Now() + WaitLogTailAppliedAt
+ UpdateSnapshot to advance the transaction snapshot. This ensures Relations() sees all tables committed before the lock was acquired.

Uses clock.Now() instead of GetLatestCommitTS() (v1) because doWrite
defer LIFO ordering causes unlock to execute before updateLastCommitTS,
making the v1 conditional check always false in the critical scenario.

Files: ddl.go, ddl_test.go

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong, @ouyuanning, @aunjgr

commit.message:
fix pr23674 (#23793)

Fix Python UDF body escaping and local Python UDF service wiring
Background
Creating a Python UDF with a $$...$$ body could fail at runtime with:

invalid character '\\' looking for beginning of object key string

This happened when calling the UDF, even though creation succeeded.

Root Cause
Double-escaping in UDF metadata storage

Python UDF body JSON was first strconv.Quote-escaped, then SQL-escaped again before writing to mo_catalog.mo_user_defined_function.body.
Stored value became {\"handler\":...} instead of valid JSON {"handler":...}.
At execution time, json.Unmarshal in Python UDF path failed.
Runtime service wiring (local launch)

Local launch config did not start Python UDF service or wire CN to Python UDF client, causing missing python udf service.
On macOS, python may be unavailable while python3 exists.
Changes
pkg/frontend/authenticate.go

For non-SQL UDFs, store raw json.Marshal(...) result directly (remove strconv.Quote step).
Keep SQL literal safety via escapeSQLStringForDoubleQuotes(...).
pkg/frontend/authenticate_test.go

Added regression test to verify:
old quoted path fails JSON round-trip,
new path round-trips and unmarshals correctly.
UDF parser behavior / tests

Keep dollar-quoted string backslash preservation behavior and update related parser expectation.
Local launch config

Add Python UDF service in launch config.
Add CN Python UDF client address.
Set Python executable to /usr/bin/python3 for local environment compatibility.
Validation
go test ./pkg/frontend -run TestEscapeSQLStringForDoubleQuotes -count=1
go test ./pkg/sql/parsers/dialect/mysql -run TestValid -count=1
Reproduced SQL flow:
no longer hits invalid character '\\' ... during UDF invocation,
body_hex now starts with 7B22... ({"...) instead of 7B5C22... ({\"...),
UDF invocation proceeds to runtime-level errors (e.g., missing imports/path), confirming serialization issue is fixed.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
feat: Implement NTILE window function (#23760)

This PR implements the MySQL `NTILE` window function, which divides an ordered partition into a specified number of approximately equal groups (buckets) and returns the bucket number for each row.

### Implementation Details

**NTILE Function Behavior:**
- Syntax: `NTILE(N) OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...)`
- Divides rows into N buckets numbered from 1 to N
- When rows don't divide evenly, extra rows are distributed to the first buckets
- Example: 10 rows with NTILE(3) → buckets of size 4, 3, 3

**Changes:**
1. **Parser Layer** - Added NTILE token and grammar rules
2. **Function Registration** - Registered NTILE as a WIN_ORDER window function
3. **Executor Implementation** - Implemented `ntileWindowExec` with bucket distribution algorithm
4. **Window Operator** - Added special handling for NTILE to evaluate bucket count parameter
5. **Tests** - Added comprehensive unit tests and verified with SQL integration tests

**Bug Fix:**
- Fixed ID conflicts in `register.go`:
- `AggIdOfStdDevSample` and `AggIdOfBitXor` both had ID -25
- `AggIdOfBitmapConstruct` and `AggIdOfBitmapOr` both had ID -29
- Reassigned IDs to ensure uniqueness

### Test Results

**Unit Tests:**
- ✅ 10 rows / 3 buckets → [1,1,1,1,2,2,2,3,3,3]
- ✅ 9 rows / 3 buckets → [1,1,1,2,2,2,3,3,3]
- ✅ 5 rows / 3 buckets → [1,1,2,2,3]

**SQL Integration Tests:**
- ✅ Basic usage with ORDER BY
- ✅ With PARTITION BY
- ✅ Edge cases (1 bucket, bucket count > row count)
- ✅ NULL values and duplicate values
- ✅ Error handling (bucket count <= 0)

### Example Usage

```sql
-- Divide employees into 3 salary groups
SELECT name, salary,
NTILE(3) OVER (ORDER BY salary) as salary_group
FROM employees;

-- Divide into 2 groups within each department
SELECT name, department, salary,
NTILE(2) OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary) as dept_group
FROM employees;
```

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong, @ouyuanning, @heni02

commit.message:
fix: resolve `TestIssue19551` flaky test due to race conditions (#23772)

Root cause: two race conditions in the test:
1. txn2 could complete insert and exit the HasInvalidService loop before AddInvalidService was called, committing before txn1.
2. Background tasks (e.g. mo_table_stats) could commit while CN was invalid, triggering markAllActiveTxnAborted before txn1, marking txn1 as aborted so it gets ErrTxnClosed instead of ErrCannotCommitOnInvalidCN.

Fix:
- Both txns complete insert before CN is marked invalid (txn1InsertedC/txn2InsertedC)
- txn1 waits for invalidMarkedC after insert, so commit fires immediately
- txn2 waits for txn1 to commit first (txn1CommittedC)
- txn1 assertion accepts both ErrCannotCommitOnInvalidCN and ErrTxnClosed

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
[Fix] DropDatabase: refresh snapshot after exclusive lock to prevent orphan records from concurrent CLONE (#23769)

When CLONE (CREATE TABLE) and DROP DATABASE execute concurrently, the lock service does not advance DROP's snapshot because CLONE only modifies mo_tables, not mo_database. This causes DROP to miss newly created tables, leaving orphan records in mo_tables that trigger OkExpectedEOB panic during checkpoint replay.

Fix: after acquiring the exclusive lock in DropDatabase, explicitly advance the snapshot to the latest commit timestamp via GetLatestCommitTS + WaitLogTailAppliedAt + UpdateSnapshot, ensuring Relations() sees all tables committed before the lock was granted.

Approved by: @ouyuanning

commit.message:
select ... for update support join tables (#23768)

Support SELECT ... FOR UPDATE with JOIN tables

Previously, SELECT ... FOR UPDATE was restricted to single-table queries.
Attempting to use it with JOIN would return:
"not support select from join table for update"

This PR removes that restriction and enables row-level pessimistic locking
across all tables involved in a JOIN query.

Core change: replaced the single-table lock target logic in bindSelectClause
with a new collectLockTargets() function that recursively traverses the plan
tree, collecting LockTarget entries from every TABLE_SCAN node it encounters.
This means INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, multi-table joins, natural joins, and
comma-separated table lists all work correctly with FOR UPDATE now.

Changes:
- pkg/sql/plan/query_builder.go: refactored FOR UPDATE lock logic, removed
the join-table error check in buildTable, added collectLockTargets().
- pkg/sql/plan/build_test.go: moved two join-for-update cases from the
should-error list to the should-pass list.
- test/distributed/cases/pessimistic_transaction/select_for_update.sql and
.result: added integration tests covering inner join, left join, three-table
join, join with update in same txn, aliased join, and natural join.

Approved by: @heni02, @ouyuanning

commit.message:
fix: prebuild composite unique lock column mapping for insert (#23765)

This PR fixes a lock-target mapping issue for INSERT on tables with composite unique keys (e.g. UNIQUE KEY (a, b)), which could lead to missed conflict detection on CN.

Problem:

During lock target construction, colName2Idx could miss __mo_index_idx_col for composite unique indexes.
The previous logic then effectively used an incorrect column index path, so CN might lock the wrong column instead of the composite unique key value.
Root cause:

__mo_index_idx_col mapping for composite unique keys was not guaranteed to be materialized before lock target generation.
Fix:

Pre-materialize the composite unique lock key (serial(...)) before lock target construction.
Populate colName2Idx for indexTable.__mo_index_idx_col in advance.
Add explicit map-hit checks when reading lock target column indexes, returning an internal error if missing (instead of silently proceeding).
Why this is better:

Moves lock-key readiness earlier in the pipeline, so lock target building reads a complete mapping.
Avoids implicit/default index behavior.
Keeps lock behavior deterministic for composite unique keys.

Approved by: @ouyuanning, @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
fix get format date (#23761)

Fix GET_FORMAT() treating DATE/TIME/DATETIME/TIMESTAMP as column names

Previously, GET_FORMAT(DATE, 'USA') failed with column DATE does not exist because the parser had no dedicated grammar rule — it fell through to the generic function call path where bare keywords like DATE were resolved as column references.

Changes:

Added get_format_type grammar rule in mysql_sql.y matching DATE/TIME/DATETIME/TIMESTAMP keywords, converting them to string constants via tree.NewNumVal
Added GET_FORMAT token, keyword entry, and placed it in func_not_keyword
Implemented GetFormat function in func_binary.go with switch-based lookup for all type×locale combinations (TIMESTAMP treated as DATETIME synonym)
Registered function overloads in list_builtIn.go for varchar/char argument combinations
Added parser tests, plan-level tests in build_test.go, unit tests in func_binary_test.go
Updated BVT tests: previously-failing bare keyword cases now return correct results; quoted strings/NULL/column refs as first arg now correctly return syntax errors (MySQL-compatible behavior)

Approved by: @ouyuanning, @heni02, @iamlinjunhong

commit.message:
fix: force CTAS follow-up insert to run on one CN (#23750)

For CTAS, table creation and data population now happen in the same transaction.
the newly created target table (including hidden auto-increment/fake-pk metadata), causing pre-insert auto-increment
lookup to fail as no such table.

## Fix

Before executing the CTAS follow-up internal SQL (createAsSelectSql), mark the current transaction as having DDL:

- pkg/sql/compile/ddl.go (normal CTAS path)
- pkg/sql/compile/ddl.go (temp table CTAS path)

This makes the internal SQL compile path choose one-CN execution, so the follow-up insert runs on the coordinator CN
and can access uncommitted metadata in the same transaction.

## Why This Is Safe

- Scope is limited to CTAS internal follow-up SQL only.
- No user-visible SQL semantics are changed.
- This is a correctness-first mitigation; only CTAS internal insert distribution is constrained.

## Impact

- Fixes CTAS failures in multi-CN for same-transaction create+insert scenarios.
- Potentially reduces parallelism for CTAS follow-up insert (expected and acceptable for correctness).

Approved by: @ouyuanning

commit.message:
implement issue 12963 (#23699)

Root Cause
We need MySQL‑compatible idle disconnect behavior for wait_timeout / interactive_timeout.
The previous behavior wasn’t observable because idle timeout wasn’t being applied consistently at read time, and tests were often run via the proxy port (which doesn’t enforce CN idle timeout). In addition, the mysql client auto‑reconnects by default, which can mask disconnections.

Fix

Apply wait_timeout as the read timeout before each request read.
For interactive clients, only at login we overwrite session.wait_timeout with interactive_timeout (one‑time override).
Disallow GLOBAL changes to wait_timeout / interactive_timeout (cloud policy).
Add session‑level min/max hard limits validation.
Added minimal unit tests covering interactive/non‑interactive behavior, session/global restrictions, and boundary checks.
Verification SQL / Steps (direct CN connection, not proxy)

Use mysql --skip-reconnect to avoid auto‑reconnect masking the disconnect.

Interactive client (should disconnect):

set session wait_timeout=3;
-- idle 6 seconds
select 1;  -- expected: ERROR 2013 / Lost connection
Interactive client (should NOT disconnect):

set session interactive_timeout=3;
-- idle 6 seconds
select 1;  -- expected: returns 1
Non‑interactive client (batch; should disconnect):

set session wait_timeout=3;
-- idle 6 seconds
select 1;  -- expected: ERROR 2013 / Lost connection

We changed the default wait_timeout and interactive_timeout from 28800 to 86400 to align with the cloud policy and login flow. In cloud mode, SET GLOBAL for these variables is blocked, but timeout values are needed before tenant login, when tenant-specific configs are not yet available. So we use 86400 (24h) as a safe pre-login fallback, and then override session values with tenant settings from mo_mysql_compatibility_mode after login.

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong, @fengttt, @XuPeng-SH, @heni02

commit.message:
fix: revert getJoinSide JoinSideBoth change and fix uniform estimation (#23745)

**Problem:** The main branch TPCH 1t test hit OOM (commit f864d27). The change "fix: getJoinSide return JoinSideBoth when column not in either join subtree" (#23719) introduced incorrect behavior that led to this regression.

**Solution:**

1. **Revert the problematic getJoinSide change**: Remove the logic that returns `JoinSideBoth` when a column's RelPos is not in leftTags or rightTags. This reverts the behavior that caused the OOM.

2. **Fix uniform estimation in ShuffleRange.Eval()**: Correct the uniform density calculation:
- Use `globalDensity := float64(s.Sz) / s.SampleRatio / (s.Max - s.Min)` to account for sampling ratio when computing density
- Set `s.Uniform = min(0.999, globalDensity/s.Uniform)` where `s.Uniform` holds max local density during iteration
- Properly propagate `actualSamplingRatio` to ShuffleRanges after stats collection

3. **Sampling adjustments**: Increase MaxSampleObjects (2000→5000) and target sampling ratio (2%→10%) for better stats accuracy on large tables.

Approved by: @aunjgr, @XuPeng-SH, @heni02

commit.message:
fix the conv bug (#23720)

CONV() was implemented to read numeric inputs via GenerateFunctionFixedTypeParameter[int64] inside convInt64Direct. This works only when the underlying vector is actually int64. When the input column is INT/SMALLINT/TINYINT (i.e., int32/int16/int8), GenerateFunctionFixedTypeParameter[int64] triggers a type mismatch and panics. The crash surfaced in func_other_conv when calling CONV(num_val, 10, 16) where num_val is INT, producing the panic type mismatch: casting INT vector to []int64 stack trace.

Impact
Any CONV() call over non-int64 integer columns (or uint*/float32) could crash the execution path. This affected BVT coverage and was marked as @bvt:issue#22963 in the test.

Fix Summary

Dispatch CONV by the actual input type, rather than assuming int64.
Add dedicated handlers for int8, int16, int32, uint8, uint16, uint32, and float32.
Share signed conversion formatting via a helper so negative values still follow MySQL behavior (convert as unsigned).
Update test expectations for the affected query and remove the @bvt:issue#22963 skip.
Files Touched

[func_binary.go](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.69-darwin-x64/webview/#)
Type-specific dispatch and per-type conversion handlers.
[func_other_conv.test](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.69-darwin-x64/webview/#)
Removed @bvt:issue#22963 markers.
[func_other_conv.result](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.69-darwin-x64/webview/#)
Replaced panic stack with correct result rows.
Verification
Executed [func_other_conv.test](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.69-darwin-x64/webview/#) against local MatrixOne. All statements completed with expected output, including the table case:

hex_val  hex_str
10       a
255      ff
4112     a
Notes / Future Considerations
This fix addresses the crash and correctness for existing CONV() tests. It does not change other MySQL compatibility gaps (e.g., negative bases or non-constant bases); those remain out of scope for this PR.

Approved by: @heni02, @aunjgr

commit.message:
fix: UT `TestPauseResumeDaemonTask` deadwait (#23732)

### Background

This PR addresses matrixone#23725, where daemon task control operations


Two issues combined to cause the hang:

1. dispatchTaskHandle could enqueue handlers while still interacting with daemonTasks state, creating lock/channel blocking cycles
under pressure.
2. Control handlers (pause/resume/restart/cancel) were not fully idempotent for duplicate/stale requests, so repeated requests could
re-enter paths that block on routine state.
### Repro (before fix)

A stable repro was built around repeated pause handling:

- TestPauseTaskHandleIdempotent with high repeat count (-count=200) could hit the dead-wait path before this fix.

### Fix

- Refactored daemon task dispatch flow to build handlers first and then enqueue, avoiding lock + channel-send blocking cycles.
- Added safer daemon task map access helper (getDaemonTask) and consistent lock boundaries.
- Added idempotent guards in control handlers:
- pause
- resume
- restart
- cancel
- Added/updated English comments in key paths.

### Testing

- Expanded task framework tests significantly (daemon/task runner/storage/service paths).
- Current package coverage:
- go test ./pkg/taskservice/... -covermode=atomic
- 90.2% of statements
- Race validation:
- go test -race ./pkg/taskservice -count=1 passed
- targeted multi-run race checks for new daemon handler tests passed

### Notes

- This does touch core task framework behavior (dispatch/control path), but changes are focused on dead-wait prevention and
idempotency with broad regression coverage added.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

fix: incremental column query order (#23721)

**Problem:** `getTableAutoIncrCol` iterated over all table defs and overwrote `autoIncrCol` whenever it saw an auto_increment attribute. That made `internal_auto_increment()` / show table status show the wrong column (e.g. the fake PK), which is confusing

commit.message:
fix: getJoinSide return JoinSideBoth when column not in either join subtree (#23719)

**Root cause:** During filter pushdown we recurse into join nodes and classify each filter with `getJoinSide(filter, leftTags, rightTags)`. `leftTags`/`rightTags` are the binding tags of *this* JOIN's left and right subtrees only. Filters are pushed from the root and can reference columns from any table in the query (outer scope). At an inner JOIN such as `JOIN(item, subquery_m)`, a filter may reference e.g. `supplier.s_suppkey` (tag 7); that tag is in neither leftTags nor rightTags because supplier is in another branch of the join tree. The old logic returned 0 for such columns, so the filter was OR'd to JoinSideLeft and pushed to the item subtree, which then failed at remap time.

**Change:** In `getJoinSide`, when a column's RelPos is not in leftTags, not in rightTags, and not markTag, return `JoinSideBoth` instead of 0. The filter is then not pushed to one child only and stays at the join (OnList/cantPushdown), avoiding remap failure.

Approved by: @heni02, @ouyuanning

commit.message:
feat: MySQL JSON operators -> and ->> plus JSON to numeric cast (#23717)

Implements MySQL-compatible JSON operators **`->`** (json_extract) and **`->>`** (json_unquote(json_extract)), and fixes/refactors JSON→numeric cast so expressions like `WHERE (c -> '$.id') > 1` work.

**Parser & grammar**
- Scanner: on `-` followed by `>`, emit LONG_ARROW if next is `>`, else ARROW.
- Grammar: `expr -> path` → `json_extract(expr, path)`; `expr ->> path` → `json_unquote(json_extract(expr, path))`.

**JSON → numeric cast (func_cast.go)**
- Supported targets: int8–int64, uint8–uint64, float32/64, decimal64/128.
- Single loop in `jsonToNumeric` with type-switch helpers `jsonAppendNull` / `jsonAppendValue` so each target uses the correct `FunctionResult[T]` (fixes "wrong type for FunctionResultWrapper" panic for INT8/INT16/etc.).
- `jsonToScalar` treats JSON object/array as invalid for numeric cast (return error instead of null).
- Decimal64 zero handled via `var dft types.Decimal64` (no composite literal).

**Tests & docs**
- Unit tests in `func_cast_test.go`: `TestCastJsonToNumeric` (success: number/string-number/null → int64/int8/float64/uint64; error: object/array/non-numeric string).
- BVT: `func_json_arrow.sql` / `.result` (column-left `->`/`->>`` only, per MySQL).
- RFC: `docs/rfcs/20260208_json_arrow_operators.md`; removed `docs/design/23006-json-arrow-operator.md`

Approved by: @heni02, @iamlinjunhong, @XuPeng-SH, @ouyuanning

commit.message:
refactor(external): introduce ExternalFileReader interface for unified file reading (#23709)

This PR refactors the External operator by introducing a unified ExternalFileReader interface, replacing the scattered if-else dispatch logic with a clean abstraction layer.

### Key changes:

New ExternalFileReader interface (types.go) with three methods: Open(), ReadBatch(), Close()

Three Reader implementations:

CsvReader - handles CSV and JSONLINE formats
ParquetReader - handles Parquet format (thin wrapper around existing ParquetHandler)
ZonemapReader - handles RESULT_SCAN (MO internal result files)
Simplified External.Prepare() and External.Call():

Single dispatch point in Prepare to create the appropriate reader
Call uses unified interface, no format-specific branching
"Open file loop" pattern to properly handle empty files
Streamlined ExParam:

Removed runtime state fields (plh, parqh, Zoneparam, prevStr, reader) - now encapsulated in respective Readers
Added AuxIdCnt to FilterParam for future RowGroup filtering support
Bug fixes:

Fixed CsvReader.Open to handle GetUnCompressReader returning (nil, nil) on EOF
Fixed ParquetHandler.openFile to support INLINE mode and prevent FileIndex out-of-bounds access

### Behavior changes:

Empty files (0-row Parquet/CSV) now skip directly via fileEmpty=true instead of returning an empty batch first

Approved by: @aunjgr

commit.message:
fix: show table status Auto_increment and related columns (#23698)

Fixes inaccurate result of `SHOW TABLE STATUS FROM db`: the Auto_increment column was always 0 for all tables.

**Changes:**
1. **Auto_increment** – In `build_show.go`, use `internal_auto_increment(reldatabase, relname)` instead of hardcoded 0. Tables with an auto_increment column now show the next value; tables without show 0; views show NULL.

commit.message:
fix: fix pipeline client leak (#23710)

### Summary
- **readLoop**: Call `active()` only for non-internal messages. Heartbeat (ping/pong) no longer updates `LastActiveTime`, so idle
GC can close backends that only receive heartbeats and avoid connection leaks.
- **getBackendLocked**: Call `maybeCreateLocked` only when no available backend was found (`b == nil`), preventing unbounded
backend growth when backends are locked/busy.

Approved by: @jiangxinmeng1

commit.message:
improvement: reduce morpc backend heap inuse (#23695)

1. Per-session newCounters (~450KB each)

morpc creates goetty IOSessions without a logger. goetty then builds a default zap logger with sampling, which allocates newCounters per session. Many
backends ⇒ tens of GB of allocations.
2. Backend not GC’d after close

Backend → pool.streams → stream (Put back) → stream holds s.rb (backend) ⇒ cycle. Backend stays reachable and heap inuse does not drop.

commit.message:
fix(taskservice): return error instead of fatal when task not found during allocation (#23697)

When allocating a task, if the task is not found (query returns 0 records), return ErrInvalidTask instead of calling Fatal. This handles the race condition where a task is completed and deleted between queryTasks and Allocate calls.

This can happen when:
1. Scheduler queries Created tasks
2. CN node picks up and completes the task quickly
3. Another scheduler cycle truncates completed tasks
4. Original scheduler tries to allocate the now-deleted task

The Fatal call was too aggressive for this normal distributed system race condition. Now the scheduler will gracefully skip the task and continue with the next scheduling cycle.

Keep Fatal only for len(exists) > 1 case which indicates a real data consistency issue.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
fix the result_scan (#23678)

Issue: column name casing was inconsistent between direct query results and result_scan. The query result metadata was saved with original column names, but GetQueryResultMeta() forced all column names to lower-case when reading them back. As a result, result_scan outputs lowercased column headers (e.g., AbC → abc), while direct queries preserved the original case.

Fix: remove the forced lowercasing in GetQueryResultMeta() and keep original column names in the result metadata path. To preserve case-insensitive column resolution, bindings still use lowercased names internally, but star expansion now uses the original column names for headings. Added a unit test to assert metadata preserves case, and a BVT case to verify result_scan returns the original column casing.

Approved by: @aunjgr, @XuPeng-SH, @heni02

commit.message:
fix python udf error (#23674)

The MySQL parser’s string scanner treats all string literals the same and applies escape handling (e.g., \n → newline). For dollar-quoted strings ([$$...$$](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.67-darwin-x64/webview/#)), this is incorrect: the content should be preserved verbatim. As a result, \\n inside a Python UDF body was converted into a real newline during parsing, breaking the Python code (e.g., "\\n" became an actual line break).

Fix (English)
Disable escape processing for dollar-quoted strings in the MySQL scanner. Specifically, when the delimiter is $, the scanner now skips handleEscape and keeps backslashes intact. This preserves sequences like \\n in UDF bodies.
Added tests to cover this:

Unit test ensures $$a\\n$$ parses to a\\n.
BVT case verifies UDF body storage retains \\n (via hex(body) check).

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong, @XuPeng-SH, @heni02

fix python udf error (#23674)

The MySQL parser’s string scanner treats all string literals the same and applies escape handling (e.g., \n → newline). For dollar-quoted strings ([$$...$$](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/Users/moukeyu/.vscode/extensions/openai.chatgpt-0.4.67-darwin-x64/webview/#)), this is incorrect: the content should be preserved verbatim. As a result, \\n inside a Python UDF body was converted into a real newline during parsing, breaking the Python code (e.g., "\\n" became an actual line break).

Fix (English)
Disable escape processing for dollar-quoted strings in the MySQL scanner. Specifically, when the delimiter is $, the scanner now skips handleEscape and keeps backslashes intact. This preserves sequences like \\n in UDF bodies.
Added tests to cover this:

Unit test ensures $$a\\n$$ parses to a\\n.
BVT case verifies UDF body storage retains \\n (via hex(body) check).

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong, @XuPeng-SH, @heni02

commit.message:
fix the issue 18862 (#23688)

Reason
load_file(datalink) reads the entire file into memory and materializes it as a single blob. For large files (e.g., 7+ GB), this leads to oversized allocations in the vector/mpool, causing OOM or “Invalid alloc size” errors. The function has no safe way to return a multi‑GB blob in a single row.

Solution
Introduce a table function load_file_chunks(datalink, chunk_size) that reads the file in bounded chunks and returns multiple rows (chunk_id, offset, data). This avoids large allocations and makes large file reads safe and predictable. We also keep the existing load_file(datalink) size guard so oversized single‑row reads fail fast with a clear error instead of crashing.

Approved by: @aunjgr

commit.message:
fix: enable timely stats update to avoid large row count estimation deviation (#23692)

After the stats refactoring, when multiple secondary indexes are created concurrently, the planner observes a smaller `Stats.OutCnt` because global stats were not updated in time on data object (block) changes. The upgrade-to-table-lock condition (`Stats.OutCnt >= MaxRowLockCount`) is then never reached, so the system keeps acquiring row locks and can accumulate ~100M row locks causing OOM (see issue #23689).

This change treats data object logtail entries (`api.Entry_DataObject`) as meta-changing in `processLogtail`, in addition to existing meta entries. When blocks/data objects change, they are counted as meta changes and can trigger stats refresh (subject to existing rate limiting in `shouldEnqueueUpdate`). Stats then reflect table size more timely and row count estimation deviation is reduced, so the planner can correctly upgrade to table lock and avoid excessive row locks.

Approved by: @gouhongshen

commit.message:
fix flaky `TestDebounceSimulatesK8sScaling` by upgrading debounce precision (#23690)

* Overview
This PR resolves the flakiness in the unit test TestDebounceSimulatesK8sScaling by upgrading the quota refresh debounce mechanism
from second-level to nanosecond-level precision.

* The Problem
The previous implementation in pkg/common/system/system.go used time.Now().Unix(), which has a resolution of only 1 second.

This created a "Clock Edge" race condition:
1. If the first refresh occurred at the very end of a second (e.g., 10:00:00.995), the stored timestamp was 10:00:00.
2. The test then slept for 10ms.
3. If the sleep crossed into the next second (e.g., 10:00:01.005), time.Now().Unix() returned 10:00:01.
4. The logic calculated now - last as 1, which satisfied the >= 1s debounce threshold.
5. The test expected the event to be debounced (returning false) but it returned true, causing a failure.

This issue was particularly prevalent in CI environments or under high system load where scheduling jitter can slightly extend the
time.Sleep duration.

* The Fix
1. Precision Upgrade: Switched from Unix() to UnixNano() for recording and comparing the last refresh time.
2. Logic Adjustment: Updated shouldRefreshQuotaConfig to compare nanosecond timestamps against the 1-second interval
(1,000,000,000ns).
3. Test Synchronization: Updated system_test.go to ensure all manual timestamp manipulations use nanosecond precision to maintain
consistency.

Validation Results
* Targeted Reproduction: Verified the failure using a script that specifically triggers the logic at the 985ms mark of a second.
* Stress Test: Ran 500 iterations of the test suite under high CPU load (8 cores at 100% utilization).
* Before Fix: Multiple failures observed in 500 runs.
* After Fix: 500/500 passed successfully.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
CBloomfilter for IVF pre-filtering integration (#23565)

1. CBloomFilter in C
2. integration with readutil.
3. HNSW filtered search with bloom filter and bitset
4. move WaitBloomFilter to sqlexec and wait bloom filter right before run SQL so that centroids computation and bloomfilter build task can run in parallel.
5. change the build bloomfilter location to ivf search.  hashbuild only send unique join keys.
6. introduce variable ivf_preload_entries to enable preload centroid bloomfilters
7. introduce variable ivf_small_centroid_threshold so that when finding closest centroids, centroid with size smaller than threshold will still be included in selected lists and total number of selected centroid will be increased by one, i.e. probe_limit + 1.

commit.message:
workaround to prevent a transaction to hold too many row-locks when parallel create indexes. (#23685)

• Background / Problem
Recent stats refactoring changed Stats.OutCnt behavior during concurrent secondary-index builds. When multiple indexes are created concurrently, the insert/backfill into the index tables now observes a smaller Stats.OutCnt, so the planner-side “upgrade to table lock” condition (Stats.OutCnt >= MaxRowLockCount) is no longer reached. As a result, the system continues to acquire row locks instead of upgrading, and with ~10 tables running concurrently this can accumulate close to ~100M row locks, driving lock metadata memory usage to ~70GB and causing OOM.

Why existing upgrade fallbacks didn’t trigger

1. Per-batch upgrade (row → range) didn’t trigger
The fallback that upgrades a single lock request to a range lock only triggers when a single batch exceeds MaxRowLockCount. In practice, the pipeline batch size is ~8192, so this condition never becomes true.

2. Per-transaction upgrade (too many held row locks → table lock) didn’t trigger in time
The per-transaction safeguard upgrades only after a very large number of locks are held (e.g. ~10M). In our workload:
- A single table may not reach the threshold before memory pressure becomes critical.
- With 10 tables building concurrently, the process can OOM before any single transaction hits the upgrade threshold.

Change
Lower the default max-row-lock-count (MaxLockRowCount) from 200000 to 20000 in pkg/lockservice/cfg.go to force earlier lock upgrades and reduce the risk of lock-metadata OOM in concurrent index build/backfill workloads.

Impact

- Acts as a pragmatic workaround for the regression introduced by the stats refactor.
- Reduces peak lock metadata by triggering lock upgrades earlier, especially under concurrent index creation.
- May increase contention in some scenarios due to earlier upgrades, but avoids catastrophic OOM under the reported workload.

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong

commit.message:
fix like error (#23670)

This change fixes MySQL-style LIKE behavior when the pattern contains escaped non-wildcard characters (e.g., \+). The previous fast-path comparison treated backslash escapes as literal characters and failed to match patterns that should be interpreted as escaped literals. The fix removes escape characters before comparison in no-wildcard LIKE optimizations and adds BVT/regression coverage for patterns such as __++% and a\+b

Approved by: @heni02, @ouyuanning, @aunjgr

commit.message:
fix: StarCount with snapshot can return 0 by counting rows in persisted appendable objects (#23681)

### Problem
`SELECT count(*) FROM t { snapshot = '...' }` could return 0 instead of the real row count when some rows lived only in persisted appendable data (already
flushed from in-memory state). CollectDataStats only used metadata for non-appendable objects and the in-memory rows btree, so it skipped those persisted
appendable rows.
### Solution
- Extend CollectDataStats to take ctx and fs, and for each visible appendable data object scan its blocks, read the commit_ts column, and count rows with
commit_ts <= snapshot (same visibility rule as elsewhere).
- Add countVisibleRowsInAppendableObject to perform this block scan and return an error on I/O failure.
- Make CollectDataStats and its callers (CountRows, CalculateTableStats) propagate errors and accept an mpool argument instead of allocating one per object.

Approved by: @aptend, @gouhongshen, @xzxiong

commit.message:
fix panic runtime error (#23654)

CH-benchmark issues a prepared statement with a tuple IN predicate, e.g.
WHERE (s_w_id, s_i_id) IN ((?,?),(?,?),(?,?),(?,?),(?,?)) FOR UPDATE.
In some cases the AST wraps the tuple with ParenExpr, so the binder fails to recognize it as a tuple and falls back to the generic =/IN binding path. That path treats the tuple as T_tuple, which is not supported by the = operator type checker, leading to invalid argument operator =, bad value [TUPLE TUPLE]. The error is not properly short‑circuited and later hits makePlan2CastExpr, causing a nil‑pointer panic during prepare.

Fix

Unwrap parentheses before tuple checks in bindComparisonExpr so ((a,b)) is treated as a tuple and consistently expanded into AND/OR comparisons instead of going through the generic =/IN path.
Defensive guard in makePlan2CastExpr to return a clean error on nil expressions instead of panicking.
Impact

Prevents the prepare‑time panic for tuple IN with parameters.
No behavior change for non‑tuple expressions; it only improves tuple recognition.
Added a unit test to cover tuple IN with extra parentheses.
Validation

Manual prepare/execute of the CH statement runs successfully (5/5 retries) after the fix.
Added test case: ((a,b)) IN (((1,2)), ((3,4))) in query_builder_test.

Approved by: @ouyuanning, @aunjgr, @heni02

commit.message:
morpc: fix backend logger memory, 10s GC for closed backends, and pipeline stream metric (#23683)

**1. Backend logger memory (backend.go)**
- Stop using Logger.With() per backend to avoid cloning the zap core chain and newCounters growth (e.g. ~83GB under sysbench).
- Use a shared logger and add logID + logFieldsCache in adjust(); all log sites use rb.logFields() or append(rb.logFields(), ...).
- Add backend tests and adjust server_test as needed.

**2. 10s GC for closed backends (client.go, client_gc.go)**
- Explicitly closed (inactive) backends (LastActiveTime == zero) are now removed from the pool within ~10s instead of waiting for the 1-minute idle timeout.

- Add client.doRemoveInactiveAll() and, in the existing GC idle goroutine, a 10s ticker that runs doGCInactivePeriodic() for all registered clients (no
extra goroutine).
- Cleanup is per-remote; only inactive backends are removed.

**3. Pipeline stream metric (remoterunClient, metrics, pipeline)**
- Replace pipeline stream Counter with a Gauge: living senders Inc() on create and Dec() on close. Counter never decreased and Desc() does not decrement, so
the metric now reflects current number of stream connections.

Approved by: @aptend, @ouyuanning

- Explicitly closed (inactive) backends (LastActiveTime == zero) are now removed from the pool within ~10s instead of waiting for the 1-minute idle timeout.

- Add client.doRemoveInactiveAll() and, in the existing GC idle goroutine, a 10s ticker that runs doGCInactivePeriodic() for all registered clients (no
extra goroutine).
- Cleanup is per-remote; only inactive backends are removed.

**3. Pipeline stream metric (remoterunClient, metrics, pipeline)**
- Replace pipeline stream Counter with a Gauge: living senders Inc() on create and Dec() on close. Counter never decreased and Desc() does not decrement, so
the metric now reflects current number of stream connections.

commit.message:
(fix): TestAbortRemoteDeadlockTxn hang and waiter ordering race condition (#23668)

**Root cause**: Waiter was added to `events.mu.blockedWaiters` (status=ready) before being set to blocking state via `setBlocked()`. This created a race window where `waiter_events.check()` could remove the waiter as "non-blocking" before `setBlocked()` completed.

**Fix**: Reorder operations to call `setBlocked()` before `events.add()`:
- Ensures waiter is in blocking state before entering `blockedWaiters`
- Prevents `check()` from incorrectly removing valid waiters
- Eliminates the race condition

Approved by: @iamlinjunhong

commit.message:
(improvement): Reduce cgroup refresh frequency with debouncing (#23655) ### **User description** ## What type of PR is this? - [ ] API-change - [ ] BUG - [x] Improvement - [ ] Documentation - [ ] Feature - [ ] Test and CI - [ ] Code Refactoring ## Which issue(s) this PR fixes: issue #20964 ## What this PR does / why we need it: ### What - Add a 1s debounce for `refreshQuotaConfig()` when watching `cpu.max` and `memory.max` in `notify_linux.go`. - On K8s vertical scaling, kubelet often updates both files in quick succession, which used to trigger two refreshes and duplicate logs. We now allow at most one refresh per second in that case. ### How - `shouldRefreshQuotaConfig()` returns true only when it’s the first refresh or ≥1s since the last one. - `refreshQuotaConfig()` updates `lastQuotaRefreshTime` at entry; both `cpuFd` and `memFd` inotify branches call it only when `shouldRefreshQuotaConfig()` is true. ### Why (fixes #20964) - Vertical autoscaling caused frequent "got cpu.max changed" / "got memory.max changed" logs and redundant quota recalculations. - Issue suggested comparing previous vs current cgroup values. This PR uses time-based debouncing instead: no extra cgroup reads, simpler logic, and the same reduction in refresh/log noise for the typical two-event burst. ___ ### **PR Type** Enhancement ___ ### **Description** - Add 1-second debouncing for cgroup quota refresh operations - Prevent duplicate refreshes during Kubernetes vertical pod autoscaling - Reduce redundant logs and quota recalculations from rapid config changes - Implement comprehensive test coverage for debounce mechanism ___ ### Diagram Walkthrough ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Cgroup file change detected"] --> B["shouldRefreshQuotaConfig check"] B -->|First refresh or 1s passed| C["refreshQuotaConfig executes"] B -->|Within 1s window| D["Skip refresh"] C --> E["Update lastQuotaRefreshTime"] E --> F["Read CPU/Memory stats"] D --> G["No action taken"] ```

implement AES_ENCRYPT() and AES_DECRYPT() (#23639)

This PR implements a minimal MySQL-compatible AES_ENCRYPT/AES_DECRYPT enhancement aligned with block_encryption_mode.
What changed:
Added 3-argument overloads (AES_ENCRYPT(str, key, iv) / AES_DECRYPT(crypt, key, iv)) for varchar/char/text/blob inputs, returning blob/varchar as appropriate.

commit.message:
fix: ensure fake PK generation before index maintenance in ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE (#23649)

Fixes the issue where `__mo_fake_pk_col` (auto-increment fake primary key) was not properly generated before index maintenance for tables without user-defined primary keys in `INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` scenarios.

In the old planner path, the update stream contains both:
- Update rows (origin row_id is NOT NULL)
- Insert-only rows (origin row_id is NULL)

The unique index update plan will also create new index entries for insert-only rows. For tables without a user primary key, these entries depend on `__mo_fake_pk_col`. We must ensure `__mo_fake_pk_col` is generated before index maintenance runs, while maintaining the full batch shape required by delete/index plans.

By adding a PRE_INSERT node in the update plan, we fill auto-increment columns in "new update" mode, ensuring fake primary key generation is completed before index maintenance.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH, @heni02, @ouyuanning

commit.message:
(improvement): startcount optimization with tombstones (#23636)

### StarCount optimization for `SELECT COUNT(*)`
• Main change: For a single SELECT COUNT(*) (or equivalent count(not_null_col)) with no filter/groupby, use a lightweight
EstimateCommittedTombstoneCount to decide path: if estimate > 5M tombstone rows, use the per-block path; otherwise use the
metadata-only StarCount() fast path to avoid expensive CollectTombstoneStats on large tombstone sets.
• Engine API: Add StarCount(ctx) and EstimateCommittedTombstoneCount(ctx) on Relation; implement in disttae, memoryengine, and
partition_state.
• Supporting: Plan/compile rewrite so count(not_null_col) and COUNT(lit) use starcount and countStarExec; fix StarCount undercount
when a batch has multiple persisted-insert ObjectStats; StarCount metrics and Grafana row; export IsDataObjectVisible.

commit.message:
fix like op get wrong result with '++' partern (#23630)

This PR fixes incorrect LIKE results when the pattern contains regex metacharacters such as +, . and others. The previous implementation converted LIKE patterns to regex but only escaped %, _, and parentheses, leaving other regex metacharacters unescaped. As a result, patterns like "__++%" were interpreted as regex quantifiers instead of literal plus signs, producing wrong matches. The fix ensures all non-wildcard characters are properly escaped when building the regex, and adds regression coverage for the reported cases.

Approved by: @aunjgr, @XuPeng-SH, @ouyuanning, @heni02

commit.message:
fix wrong result of currval (#23629)

nextval() updates session state via SeqAddValues, but the session only merges those values into SeqCurValues on SELECT response paths. For non-SELECT statements (e.g., INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE), the merge never happens, so currval() reads a stale value.
Fix:
Synchronize sequence session state after all statement types, not just SELECT. On response finalization, merge SeqAddValues into session and refresh SeqLastValue, ensuring currval() reflects nextval() even when called inside non-SELECT statements. Added a regression test and updated the existing sequence result case for issue #9847.

Approved by: @heni02, @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
proxy: distinguish busy CN servers from unavailable CN servers (#23606)

When all CN servers fail due to handshake timeout (typically caused by too many active transactions), return a more specific error message 'all CN servers are busy, possibly due to too many active transactions' instead of the generic 'no available CN server'.

This helps users understand that CN servers are actually running but overloaded, rather than being down or unreachable.

Changes:
- Add timeout flag to connectErr to track timeout failures
- Add newTimeoutConnectErr() for creating timeout-specific errors
- Add isTimeoutErr() to check if error is timeout-related
- Track timeout CN servers separately in BuildConnWithServer()
- Return allCNServersBusyErr when all failures are due to timeout
- Add test case for timeout error handling

Approved by: @xzxiong, @volgariver6, @gouhongshen, @XuPeng-SH

fix: return UUID as string type for MySQL clients (#23625)

The server was returning UUID values as strings, but the MySQL column type was still MYSQL_TYPE_UUID. Go MySQL drivers (e.g. usql) do not recognize this type and treated it as raw bytes, displaying Base64 or failing when the row encoder tried to read UUIDs as byte slices.

Fix: We downgraded the exposed column type for types.T_uuid to MYSQL_TYPE_VAR_STRING and updated the MySQL row encoding paths to handle UUIDs as strings under VAR_STRING. Added regression coverage to ensure UUIDs are encoded correctly with VAR_STRING and updated distributed SQL/result cases.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH, @heni02

commit.message:
Fix UNION with ORDER BY FIELD() function (#23626)

This PR fixes a critical bug where UNION/UNION ALL queries fail when ORDER BY references result columns within function calls (e.g., `ORDER BY FIELD(name, ...)`). The query would incorrectly report "column does not exist" error.

### Root Cause Analysis

The bug stems from two interconnected issues in the query builder's UNION handling:

**Issue 1: Incorrect Node Creation Order**
- The PROJECT node was created before ORDER BY binding
- This caused ORDER BY expressions to be missing from the PROJECT node's output
- When SORT node tried to reference these expressions, they were unavailable

**Issue 2: Incomplete Column Resolution in UNION Context**
- When ORDER BY uses a UNION result column inside a function (e.g., `FIELD(name, ...)`), the column resolution fails
- In UNION context, columns exist in `aliasMap` but not in `bindingByCol`
- The `aliasMap` entries have `astExpr = nil` (UNION doesn't preserve original AST)
- The binder lacked fallback logic to resolve such column references

Approved by: @heni02, @ouyuanning

commit.message:
disable wrong parallel decompression for LOAD DATA (#23620)

Summary

Parallel LOAD DATA on compressed files is unsafe because the current implementation splits input by byte offsets and then tries to locate line boundaries on a compressed stream. This can cut rows in the middle, cause column misalignment, and finally write corrupted values (e.g., invalid DATE/DECIMAL). The issue manifests later as panics during query output (e.g., Date.ToBytes out-of-range).

Root Cause

ReadFileOffset computes offsets using Offset = previous + batchSize and then getTailSize on the compressed reader, which is not a valid line-boundary strategy for compressed streams. This leads to row corruption.

Fix (medium-cost path)

Keep parallel write, but disable parallel splitting for compressed files.
For compressed data, load is handled by a single reader (proper decompression + parsing), and the insert stage still runs in parallel.
Avoid the “string vector + cast projection” path for compressed files to prevent misalignment.
Tests

Added a unit test that asserts ReadFileOffset rejects compressed input.
This locks the unsafe behavior and prevents regression.
Note: If we later refactor to truly support parallel splitting for compressed files (e.g., with a splittable format), this test must be updated or removed.
Behavior After Fix

Compressed LOAD DATA no longer panics.
Parallelism is preserved on the write side; read-side splitting is disabled for compressed inputs.

Approved by: @aunjgr

commit.message:
Fix: propagate pState from `ObjListRelData` to embedded `BlockListRelData` in `BuildEmptyRelData` (#23615)

ObjListRelData.BuildEmptyRelData delegates to its embedded blocklistRelData without first copying PState. This causes the
returned RelData to have nil pState, triggering RELDATA-WITH-EMPTY-PSTATE warning and potentially incorrect query results when memory-resident tombstones exist.

Approved by: @XuPeng-SH

commit.message:
Handle empty parquet files by checking column count only (#23601) (#23607)

For empty parquet files (0 rows), only check column count instead of column names/types. This aligns with DuckDB behavior and fixes column xxx not found errors when loading empty parquet files with different column names.

Approved by: @aunjgr, @heni02

releases.name: MatrixOne-v3.0.6
releases.body:
Release date: Jan 28 2026
MatrixOne version: v3.0.6

MatrixOne 3.0.6  introduces  improvements and bug fixes that enhance  usability. Below are the major updates.

## Key Improvements
1. Bloom filter & multi‑CN: Support Bloom filters without regular filters and broadcast Bloom filters across CNs to improve distributed query performance.
2. Backup & export: Add a file index for backups and fix table data export issues to make backup/restore and export more reliable.
3. Vector features: Enable simsimd (MO_CL_SIMSIMD=1), support vector indexes for prepared statements, and refine vector test cases for better performance and stability.
4. Optimizer & execution: Improve UNION result stability, refine filter push‑down and hint handling (including CTE and type matching), and correct NDV estimation for modulo operations.
5. GC & storage: Optimize GC behavior, fix tmp‑fs GC logging nil‑entry handling, and scope CN segment maps by transaction and table to avoid clone‑related delete skips.

## Bug Fixes
1. Fix a hang that could occur after packet loss during data loading.
2. Fix inconsistent caching in the auto‑increment service, stabilize nested SQL/auto‑increment retry paths, and remove noisy auto‑increment logs.
3. Fix multi‑CN scan statistics consistency, a window function compatibility issue, and cap out of range crashes.
4. Correct default MARCH for x86_64 (was incorrectly set to haswell) to avoid compatibility and performance issues.

## What's Changed
* modify year from 2025 to 2026. by @Ariznawlll in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23441
* use bf without filter and broadcast bf with multi cn by @iamlinjunhong in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23446
* alter cases by @heni02 in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23466
* Add file index for backup  by @LeftHandCold in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23474

* remove a useless warning 3.0 by @aptend in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23482
* Fixed a bug related to inconsistent caching in the auto-increment service by @LeftHandCold in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23489
* to 3.0: Fix multi-CN scan collection consistency and document AP shuffle/merg… by @gouhongshen in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23492
* enable simsimd library with MO_CL_SIMSIMD=1 by @cpegeric in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23490

* chore: change code owner 3.0-dev by @XuPeng-SH in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23500

* order for union for stable test by @iamlinjunhong in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23505
* to 3.0: Stabilize retry paths for nested SQL and auto‑increment allocation by @gouhongshen in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/23343

releases.name: MatrixOne-v3.0.5
releases.body:
Release date: Jan 5 2026
MatrixOne version: v3.0.5

MatrixOne 3.0.5  introduces  improvements and bug fixes that enhance  usability. Below are the major updates.

## Key Improvements

### Vector Search & AI Features
- **Vector Index Enhancements**: Removed experimental flags for IVF and FullText indexes, making them production-ready
- **Vector Pre-filtering**: Added support for enable_vector_prefilter_by_default variable and IVF pre-filtering
- **Vector Distance Output**: Direct vector distance output by Reader for better performance
- **Normalized L2 Support**: Allow normalized_l2 to use vector index

### Data Branch & Git4Data
- **Data Branch Operations**: Full support for data branch operations including create, diff, and stage
- **Database/Table Management**: Support for database/table deletion in data branch
- **Parallel Processing**: Data branch diff parallel writing to remote storage
- **Context-aware Keywords**: Make data-branch keywords context-aware

### CDC (Change Data Capture)
- **CDC Reimplementation**: Complete CDC reimplement based on existing design
- **GC Support**: GC now supports CDC functionality
- **Watermark Management**: Improved CDC watermark handling and cleanup
- **Metric Dashboard**: Added CDC metric dashboard for monitoring

### Performance & Optimization
- **Index Selection**: Optimized index selection algorithms
- **LIMIT BY RANK**: Performance optimization for LIMIT BY RANK queries
- **FullText Pushdown**: FullText pushdown limit when no filter
- **Query Metrics**: Added ReadSize, S3ReadSize, and DiskReadSize metrics to EXPLAIN ANALYZE

### Infrastructure & Development
- **Role Management**: Support for rename role name functionality
- **Multi-CN Development**: Added multi-cn docker compose file for better development
- **Go Version**: Upgraded Go version to 1.25.4
- **Logging Optimization**: Removed/optimized logging to reduce noise

## Key Bug Fixes

### Critical Panics & Crashes
- **Multi-CN IVF Search**: Fixed panic during multi-cn ivf search
- **JOIN Condition Conversion**: Fixed panic when JOIN condition type conversion fails
- **COUNT DISTINCT**: Fixed panic when using count(distinct(<col1>, <col2>))
- **ORDER BY in JOIN**: Fixed ORDER BY panic in JOIN queries
- **Remote Workspace**: Fixed remote run workspace panic
- **Insert Ignore**: Fixed insert ignore panic

### Data Consistency Issues
- **Multi-update Null Mask**: Fixed null mask mutation during shrink in multi_update
- **Data Inconsistency**: Fixed data inconsistency due to stale lock service flags
- **Index Table Consistency**: Fixed index table data inconsistency in INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
- **Stale Read**: Multiple fixes for stale read issues
- **Batch Reuse**: Fixed data mapping error when batch reused with different Vecs/Attrs

### CDC-specific Fixes
- **CDC Data Loss**: Fixed CDC pause/resume/restart data loss
- **CDC Restart**: Fixed downstream "delete" loss after CDC upstream restart
- **CDC Watermark**: Fixed CDC watermark updater and stale watermark cleanup
- **CDC Stale Read**: Fixed CDC stale read issues

### SQL & Query Fixes
- **Foreign Key Actions**: Correctly format foreign key actions in ConstructCreateTableSQL
- **Column Pruning**: Fixed column pruning failure when applying associative law rules
- **JOIN Remap**: Fixed remap error in JOIN with cross-table column references
- **Left JOIN OR**: Fixed left join OR predicate regression
- **Affected Rows**: Fixed delete/replace statement reporting incorrect affected rows

### Type Conversion & Casting
- **TEXT to CHAR/VARCHAR**: Skip length validation when casting TEXT to CHAR/VARCHAR
- **TIMESTAMP Parsing**: LOAD DATA now uses session timezone for TIMESTAMP parsing
- **Fractional Precision**: Enforce fractional-second precision end-to-end for TIME/DATETIME

### Infrastructure Fixes
- **Autocommit Sync**: Fixed autocommit status sync issue
- **Connection Balancing**: Fixed proxy connection balancing when connecting too fast
- **Client Pool**: Fixed client pool random CI errors
- **Version Information**: Fixed lost version information when building images

## What's Changed
* (pick to 3.0-dev): fix slience error by @XuPeng-SH in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/22769
* (pick to 3.0-dev): add multi-cn docker compose file for better development by @XuPeng-SH in https://github.com/matrixorigin/matrixone/pull/22766

What is MatrixOne?

MatrixOne is the industry's first database to bring Git-style version control to data, combined with MySQL compatibility, AI-native capabilities, and cloud-native architecture.

At its core, MatrixOne is a HTAP (Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing) database with a hyper-converged HSTAP engine that seamlessly handles transactional (OLTP), analytical (OLAP), full-text search, and vector search workloads in a single unified system—no data movement, no ETL, no compromises.

🎬 Git for Data - The Game Changer

Just as Git revolutionized code management, MatrixOne revolutionizes data management. Manage your database like code:

  • 📸 Instant Snapshots - Zero-copy snapshots in milliseconds, no storage explosion
  • ⏰ Time Travel - Query data as it existed at any point in history
  • 🔀 Branch & Merge - Test migrations and transformations in isolated branches
  • ↩️ Instant Rollback - Restore to any previous state without full backups
  • 🔍 Complete Audit Trail - Track every data change with immutable history

Why it matters: Data mistakes are expensive. Git for Data gives you the safety net and flexibility developers have enjoyed with Git—now for your most critical asset: your data.

🎯 Built for the AI Era

🗄️ MySQL-Compatible

Drop-in replacement for MySQL. Use existing tools, ORMs, and applications without code changes. Seamless migration path.

🤖 AI-Native

Built-in vector search (IVF/HNSW) and full-text search. Build RAG apps and semantic search directly—no external vector databases needed.

☁️ Cloud-Native

Storage-compute separation. Deploy anywhere. Elastic scaling. Kubernetes-native. Zero-downtime operations.

🚀 One Database for Everything

The typical modern data stack:

🗄️ MySQL for transactions → 📊 ClickHouse for analytics → 🔍 Elasticsearch for search → 🤖 Pinecone for AI

The problem: 4 databases · Multiple ETL jobs · Hours of data lag · Sync nightmares

MatrixOne replaces all of them:

🎯 One database with native OLTP, OLAP, full-text search, and vector search. Real-time. ACID compliant. No ETL.

⚡️ Get Started in 60 Seconds

1️⃣ Launch MatrixOne

docker run -d -p 6001:6001 --name matrixone matrixorigin/matrixone:latest

2️⃣ Create Database

mysql -h127.0.0.1 -P6001 -p111 -uroot -e "create database demo"

3️⃣ Connect & Query

Install Python SDK:

pip install matrixone-python-sdk

Vector search:

from matrixone import Client
from matrixone.orm import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Text
from matrixone.sqlalchemy_ext import create_vector_column

# Create client and connect
client = Client()
client.connect(database='demo')

# Define model using MatrixOne ORM
Base = declarative_base()

class Article(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'articles'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    title = Column(String(200), nullable=False)
    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    embedding = create_vector_column(8, "f32")

# Create table using client API
client.create_table(Article)

# Insert some data using client API
articles = [
    {'title': 'Machine Learning Guide',
     'content': 'Comprehensive machine learning tutorial...',
     'embedding': [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.12, 0.22]},
    {'title': 'Python Programming',
     'content': 'Learn Python programming basics',
     'embedding': [0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.25, 0.35, 0.45, 0.22, 0.32]},
]
client.batch_insert(Article, articles)

client.vector_ops.create_ivf(
    Article,
    name='idx_embedding',
    column='embedding',
    lists=100,
    op_type='vector_l2_ops'
)

query_vector = [0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.25, 0.35, 0.45, 0.22, 0.32]
results = client.query(
    Article.title,
    Article.content,
    Article.embedding.l2_distance(query_vector).label("distance"),
).filter(Article.embedding.l2_distance(query_vector) < 0.1).execute()
for row in results.rows:
    print(f"Title: {row[0]}, Content: {row[1][:50]}...")

# Cleanup
client.drop_table(Article)  # Use client API
client.disconnect()

Fulltext Search:

...
from matrixone.sqlalchemy_ext import boolean_match

# Create fulltext index using SDK 
client.fulltext_index.create(
    Article,name='ftidx_content',columns=['title', 'content']
)

# Boolean search with must/should operators
results = client.query(
    Article.title,
    Article.content,
    boolean_match('title', 'content')
        .must('machine')
        .must('learning')
        .must_not('basics')
).execute()

# Results is a ResultSet object
for row in results.rows:
    print(f"Title: {row[0]}, Content: {row[1][:50]}...")
...

That's it! 🎉 You're now running a production-ready database with Git-like snapshots, vector search, and full ACID compliance.

💡 Want more control? Check out the Installation & Deployment section below for production-grade installation options.

🛠️ Installation & Deployment

MatrixOne supports multiple installation methods. Choose the one that best fits your needs:

🐳 Local Multi-CN Development

Run a complete distributed cluster locally with multiple CN nodes, load balancing, and easy configuration management.

# Quick start
make dev-build && make dev-up

# Connect via proxy (load balanced)
mysql -h 127.0.0.1 -P 6001 -u root -p111

# Configure specific service (interactive editor)
make dev-edit-cn1          # Edit CN1 config
make dev-restart-cn1       # Restart only CN1 (fast!)

📖 Complete Development Guide → - Comprehensive guide covering standalone setup, multi-CN clusters, monitoring, metrics, configuration, and all make dev-* commands

🎯 Using mo_ctl Tool (Recommended for Production)

One-command deployment and lifecycle management with the official mo_ctl tool. Handles installation, upgrades, backups, and health monitoring automatically.

📖 Complete mo_ctl Installation Guide →

⚙️ Building from Source

Build MatrixOne from source for development, customization, or contributing. Requires Go 1.22, GCC/Clang, Git, and Make.
